2605.31284.title: SAM for Robust Mitochondria Instance Segmentation in Fluorescence Microscopy
2605.31284.paper_summary: The morphological analysis of mitochondria in fluorescence microscopy (FM) is crucial for understanding cellular health, energy production, and metabolic regulation. While foundation models like the Segment Anything Model (SAM) have revolutionized natural image segmentation, their direct application to FM is hindered by a significant domain shift characterized by diffraction-limited resolution, low contrast, and complex overlapping organelle networks. Furthermore, the development of robust models is bottlenecked by a severe lack of high-quality, manually annotated instance segmentation datasets for mitochondria. In this paper, we propose a scalable solution to this data scarcity by finetuning SAM exclusively on synthetically generated FM data. We simulate realistic mitochondria data and emulate the optical properties of fluorescence microscopes to create a large-scale annotated dataset. We evaluate our fine-tuned model on a curated dataset of real, manually annotated FM images. Qualitative and quantitative analyses demonstrate that our synthetically fine-tuned model improves precision and average dice score over strong baselines. This work establishes the potential of simulation-assisted training for FM instance segmentation.
2605.31284.paper_summary_zh: 線粒體在螢光顯微鏡（FM）中的形態分析對於理解細胞健康、能量產生和代謝調節至關重要。雖然像Segment Anything Model（SAM）這樣的基礎模型已經徹底改變了自然圖像分割，但其在FM中的直接應用受到由衍射限制分辨率、低對比度和複雜重疊細胞器網絡所特徵的顯著領域轉變的阻礙。此外，穩健模型的開發受到高質量、手動標註的線粒體實例分割數據集的嚴重缺乏的瓶頸。在本文中，我們提出了一種可擴展的解決方案，以解決這一數據稀缺問題，通過專門在合成生成的FM數據上微調SAM。我們模擬現實的線粒體數據，並模擬螢光顯微鏡的光學特性，以創建一個大規模的標註數據集。我們在一個經過精心挑選的真實手動標註FM圖像數據集上評估我們微調的模型。定性和定量分析表明，我們合成微調的模型在精度和平均骰子分數上超過了強基線。這項工作確立了模擬輔助訓練在FM實例分割中的潛力。
2605.31284.author: Suyog Jadhav et.al.
2605.31284.authors: Suyog Jadhav, Dilip K. Prasad, Krishna Agarwal
